guidelines.title: Chemotherapy-Induced Nausea and Vomiting Prevention
guidelines.content:
CHEMOTHERAPY-INDUCED NAUSEA AND VOMITING (CINV) PREVENTION

EMETOGENIC RISK CATEGORIES:

High Emetogenic Potential (HEC) >90%:
- Cisplatin, Carmustine >250 mg/m², Cyclophosphamide >1500 mg/m²
- Dacarbazine, Mechlorethamine, Streptozocin
- AC regimen (Anthracycline + Cyclophosphamide)

Moderate Emetogenic Potential (MEC) 30-90%:
- Carboplatin, Cyclophosphamide <1500 mg/m², Doxorubicin, Epirubicin
- Ifosfamide, Irinotecan, Oxaliplatin, Temozolomide

Low Emetogenic Potential (LEC) 10-30%:
- Docetaxel, Etoposide, 5-Fluorouracil, Gemcitabine
- Methotrexate, Paclitaxel, Pemetrexed, Topotecan

Minimal Emetogenic Potential <10%:
- Bevacizumab, Bleomycin, Fludarabine, Rituximab, Vincristine

PREVENTION REGIMENS:

High Emetogenic Chemotherapy (HEC):
4-Drug Regimen:
- NK1 antagonist:
  - Aprepitant 125 mg PO day 1, 80 mg days 2-3 OR
  - Fosaprepitant 150 mg IV day 1 only OR
  - Netupitant/Palonosetron (Akynzeo) 300/0.5 mg PO day 1 OR
  - Rolapitant 180 mg PO day 1 (single dose)
- 5-HT3 antagonist:
  - Ondansetron 8-16 mg IV or 16-24 mg PO day 1 OR
  - Granisetron 1-2 mg PO or 0.01 mg/kg IV day 1 OR
  - Palonosetron 0.25 mg IV or 0.5 mg PO day 1
- Dexamethasone:
  - Day 1: 12 mg IV/PO
  - Days 2-4: 8 mg daily (with NK1 agent)
- Olanzapine (optional but recommended):
  - 10 mg PO days 1-4
  - Reduces breakthrough nausea significantly
  - Avoid if elderly or sedation concern

Moderate Emetogenic Chemotherapy (MEC):
- 5-HT3 antagonist day 1
- Dexamethasone 8-12 mg day 1, may continue days 2-3
- Consider NK1 agent for selected regimens (carboplatin, cyclophosphamide, irinotecan)

Low Emetogenic Chemotherapy (LEC):
- Dexamethasone 8-12 mg day 1 OR
- 5-HT3 antagonist day 1 OR
- Metoclopramide 10-20 mg PO

Minimal Emetogenic Chemotherapy:
- No routine antiemetics
- PRN antiemetics available

BREAKTHROUGH NAUSEA/VOMITING:

- Use agent from different class than prophylaxis
- Options:
  - Prochlorperazine 10 mg PO/IV q6h
  - Metoclopramide 10-40 mg PO/IV q6h
  - Lorazepam 0.5-2 mg PO/IV q6h
  - Haloperidol 1-2 mg PO/IV q6h
  - Olanzapine 5-10 mg PO daily
  - Dronabinol 5 mg PO q4-6h

SPECIFIC DRUG DOSES:

Ondansetron:
- IV: 8-16 mg (max 16 mg per dose due to QTc)
- PO: 16-24 mg prior to chemo
- Maximum: 16 mg IV single dose

Granisetron:
- IV: 1 mg or 0.01 mg/kg
- PO: 2 mg once or 1 mg twice daily
- Transdermal: 3.1 mg/24h patch (apply 24-48h before chemo)

Palonosetron:
- IV: 0.25 mg
- PO: 0.5 mg
- Longer half-life (40h), effective for delayed CINV

Aprepitant:
- Day 1: 125 mg PO 1 hour before chemo
- Days 2-3: 80 mg PO each morning
- CYP3A4 inhibitor - check interactions (warfarin, steroids)

Fosaprepitant:
- 150 mg IV over 20-30 min day 1 only
- Equivalent to 3-day oral aprepitant

Dexamethasone:
- HEC: 12 mg day 1, 8 mg days 2-4
- With NK1 agent: Reduce dose (increased dex levels)
- Without NK1: 12 mg day 1, 8 mg days 2-4

Olanzapine:
- 10 mg PO days 1-4
- Or 5 mg if concerned about sedation
- Excellent for refractory/breakthrough nausea
- Avoid with other CNS depressants

ANTICIPATORY NAUSEA:
- Behavioral: Relaxation, distraction
- Lorazepam 0.5-2 mg PO night before and morning of
- Prevent by good initial antiemetic control

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ASCO/NCCN CINV Guidelines)

guidelines.title: Growth Factor Support Dosing Guidelines
guidelines.content:
GROWTH FACTOR SUPPORT DOSING GUIDELINES

G-CSF (GRANULOCYTE COLONY-STIMULATING FACTOR):

Filgrastim (Neupogen) and Biosimilars:
- Prophylaxis: 5 mcg/kg/day SC starting 24-72 hours after chemo
- Continue until ANC recovery (usually 10-14 days)
- Round to nearest vial size (300 or 480 mcg)
- Typical adult dose: 300-480 mcg daily

Pegfilgrastim (Neulasta) and Biosimilars:
- 6 mg SC once per chemotherapy cycle
- Administer 24-72 hours after chemotherapy
- On-body injector (Neulasta Onpro): Delivers ~27 hours after application
- NOT for regimens <14 days cycle length

Biosimilar Options:
- Zarxio (filgrastim-sndz)
- Nivestym (filgrastim-aafi)
- Granix (tbo-filgrastim)
- Udenyca (pegfilgrastim-cbqv)
- Fulphila (pegfilgrastim-jmdb)
- Ziextenzo (pegfilgrastim-bmez)

Indications for Primary Prophylaxis:
- Regimen with ≥20% febrile neutropenia risk
- Patient risk factors (age >65, poor PS, prior FN, extensive prior treatment)
- Treatment intent: Curative/adjuvant requires full dose intensity

Secondary Prophylaxis:
- After episode of febrile neutropenia
- After dose-limiting neutropenia

ERYTHROPOIETIN-STIMULATING AGENTS (ESAs):

Epoetin alfa (Procrit, Epogen):
- 150 units/kg SC three times weekly OR
- 40,000 units SC weekly
- If no response after 4 weeks: Increase to 300 units/kg TIW or 60,000 units weekly
- Target Hgb: 10-12 g/dL (avoid >12)
- Hold if Hgb >12, resume at 25% dose reduction when <11

Darbepoetin alfa (Aranesp):
- 2.25 mcg/kg SC weekly OR
- 500 mcg SC every 3 weeks
- Dose reduce 40% if Hgb increases >1 g/dL in 2 weeks
- Hold if Hgb ≥11 g/dL, resume 40% reduced when <10

Indications:
- Chemotherapy-induced anemia in non-curative setting
- Hgb <10 g/dL
- Avoid in curative-intent treatment (thrombosis risk)

Black Box Warnings:
- Increased mortality and tumor progression in some cancers
- Increased thromboembolism risk
- Not for Hgb >12 g/dL
- Not for curative chemotherapy regimens

THROMBOPOIETIN RECEPTOR AGONISTS:

Romiplostim (Nplate):
- ITP: 1 mcg/kg SC weekly, adjust by 1 mcg/kg to target platelets 50,000-200,000
- Maximum: 10 mcg/kg weekly
- Not routinely used for chemotherapy-induced thrombocytopenia

Eltrombopag (Promacta):
- ITP: 50 mg PO daily (25 mg in East Asians)
- Adjust to target platelets 50,000-200,000
- Take on empty stomach
- Separate from antacids, calcium, iron by 4 hours

OPRELVEKIN (IL-11) - Limited Use:
- 50 mcg/kg SC daily
- Start 6-24 hours after chemo
- Continue until platelets ≥50,000
- Fluid retention, cardiac side effects limit use

IRON SUPPLEMENTATION WITH ESAs:

IV Iron (preferred for ESA patients):

Iron sucrose (Venofer):
- 200 mg IV over 2-5 min x5 doses (1000 mg total)
- OR 300 mg IV over 1.5 hours x3 doses
- Maximum 200 mg per session

Ferric gluconate (Ferrlecit):
- 125 mg IV over 1 hour
- Typical course: 8 doses (1000 mg total)

Iron dextran (INFeD):
- Test dose: 25 mg IV over 1-2 min
- Then: Remainder of calculated dose over 1-2 hours
- Highest anaphylaxis risk

Ferric carboxymaltose (Injectafer):
- 750 mg IV x2 doses, separated by 7+ days (for weight ≥50 kg)
- Or 15 mg/kg x2 doses if <50 kg
- Maximum 1500 mg total

Iron isomaltoside/Ferric derisomaltose (Monoferric):
- 1000 mg IV single dose over 20+ min
- Or 20 mg/kg single dose if <50 kg
- Can give entire course in one visit

MONITORING:

G-CSF:
- CBC 2-3 times weekly during nadir period
- ANC recovery target ≥1000-1500/mcL

ESAs:
- Hgb weekly until stable
- Then every 2-4 weeks
- Iron studies (ferritin, TSAT) at baseline and periodically

EVIDENCE LEVEL: Level I (ASCO/NCCN Guidelines)
